easy.title: Word Formation: A Trip to the Mountains
easy.instructions: Read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap. Choose the correct answer (FCE-style, guided).
easy.passage: Last summer we had a _____ holiday in the Alps. The _____ was amazing, and the weather was _____ every day. We went walking in the mountains and the _____ from the top was incredible. The _____ we met were very friendly and gave us lots of _____ about places to visit. I would _____ the area to anyone who loves nature. The _____ of reaching the summit made all the climbing worthwhile.

gaps.explanation: Noun: view (what you see from the top).
Noun (plural): locals (people who live in the area).

medium.title: Word Formation: Learning a New Language
medium.instructions: Read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap. Use only one word in each gap (FCE Part 3).
medium.passage: Many people find it _____ to learn a new language as an adult. However, with _____ and regular practice, it is _____ possible to make progress. Some learners prefer to study _____, while others enjoy the _____ aspect of a class. There is no single _____ method; what works for one person may not work for another. The key is to stay _____ and to accept that _____ is not necessary to communicate well.

hard.title: Word Formation: The Future of Work
hard.instructions: Read the text below. Use the word given in capitals to form a word that fits in the gap. Use only one word in each gap (CAE Part 3).
hard.passage: The _____ (NATURE) of work is changing rapidly due to technology and globalisation. _____ (EMPLOY) patterns have shifted towards more flexible arrangements, and _____ (REMOTE) working has become widespread. While this can lead to greater _____ (AUTONOMOUS) for workers, it also raises questions about job _____ (SECURE) and the _____ (DISTRIBUTE) of opportunities. _____ (CRITIC) argue that not everyone has the _____ (RESOURCE) or skills to adapt, and that policymakers must address these _____ (EQUAL) to ensure a fair transition.

expert.title: Word Formation: Scientific Discovery
expert.instructions: Read the text below. Use the word given in capitals to form a word that fits in the gap. Use only one word in each gap (CPE Part 3).
expert.passage: Scientific _____ (INQUIRE) has always been driven by a combination of _____ (CURIOUS) and _____ (PRAGMATIC) concerns. While some research is motivated by _____ (THEORY) interest, much of it is funded with _____ (COMMERCE) or _____ (GOVERN) support. The _____ (RELATE) between pure and applied science is complex, and _____ (SCHOLAR) continue to debate the extent to which _____ (ETHIC) considerations should influence the direction of research.

gaps.explanation: Noun as modifier: government support (standard phrase). Governmental is possible but less common here.
